p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, stable posture.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained consistent throughout the sequence; no significant growth or decline observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Stable; no signs of chlorosis or wilting.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, two primary leaves, secondary sprouts present.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Suffered significant decline between T-4 and T-3; foliage collapsed and appears to have undergone necrosis.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Critical; high probability of plant mortality due to rapid tissue collapse.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Leaf posture remains consistent relative to the rabbit anchor; no significant movement.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot, small sprout near rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remained largely static; no visible expansion of leaf surface area.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Dormant or slow-growth phase; no signs of active distress.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Introduction of white granular material (likely perlite or fertilizer) observed starting at T-4, persisting through current.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Minimal; desk surface remains clear of organic matter.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_4: Initial state stable; transition to T-4 shows introduction of white granules.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_3: Significant collapse of p2 foliage; rapid loss of turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_3_to_T_minus_1: Stasis; no further degradation of p2, but no recovery.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing a localized failure in p2. The introduction of white granules at T-4 correlates with the rapid decline of p2, suggesting potential chemical shock or over-fertilization.
vision_report.anomalies: The sudden collapse of p2 between T-4 and T-3 is the primary anomaly. The white granules are a non-biological addition to the soil surface.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is largely stable with the exception of the Mexican Mint (p2), which underwent a rapid, likely fatal, collapse following the introduction of soil additives. The Pothos (p3) and String of Nickels (p1) remain healthy and unaffected by the environmental changes impacting p2.
